# Clooks

> A hook runtime for AI coding agents. Write hooks in TypeScript with typed contracts, not bash scripts. Starting with Claude Code; cross-agent support for Cursor Agent, Codex, OpenCode, and OpenClaw is planned. Today clooks works in any IDE that hosts Claude Code (VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, JetBrains).

Clooks sits between the native hook system of an AI coding agent and user-defined hook scripts. A small bash entrypoint in the agent's config invokes a compiled Bun binary (~15ms) that reads `.clooks/clooks.yml` and runs matching TypeScript hooks. Errors block by default: a crashed hook blocks the agent's action rather than silently passing through.

Hooks are one `.ts` file each, export a `meta` descriptor (name, events, typed config schema) plus a default handler, and are installed from a marketplace into `.clooks/vendor/`. Because vendored hooks are committed to the repo, cloning gives you the hook setup with no network step.

Supported platforms: macOS and Linux. Windows is deferred.

## Links

- [Landing page](https://clooks.cc/): product overview, install command, hook examples, FAQ
- [GitHub: clooks](https://github.com/codestripes-dev/clooks): source for the CLI and runtime
- [GitHub: clooks-marketplace](https://github.com/codestripes-dev/clooks-marketplace): registry index + curated hook packs
